DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-12-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lee Szor, Esq., 415.630.5701, specially appearing for plaintiff Lisa Ashby.
Arameh O'Boyle, Esq., 213.426.6900, for defendant Stryker Corporation.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling with modification: DEFENDANT STRYKER CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Defendant's Demurrer to the entire Amended Complaint on the ground of uncertainty is overruled. Plaintiff alleges that her injury is attributable to a single product which is sufficiently described in the pleadings. Further, nothing in Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical Co., Inc., ((1991) 21 Cal. 4th 71) or any other case cited by Defendant establishes that further specificity in the identification of the product-such as a serial or catalogue number-is required. Additionally, each cause of action incorporates Plaintiff's allegations that Stryker is the manufacturer and McKesson is the distributor, thereby giving Defendant notice of the capacity in which each cause of action is brought against it. Defendant's Demurrer to the first cause of action is moot in light of Plaintiff's concession to Defendant's Motion to Strike and the Demurrer to the second cause of action is sustained with thirty days leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts that identify what aspect of the product make it defective. The Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action, and is sustained with thirty days leave to amend for the fifth cause of action for Plaintiff to allege what statements were made that constituted the express warranty, who made them, and to which defendant they are attributable. Finally, with respect to the sixth, seventh, and eighth causes of action, the Demurrer is sustained with thirty days leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege with particularity facts that show how, when, where, to whom, and by what means the representations were tendered in order to satisfy the heightened pleading standard required for fraud claims. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-12-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lee Szor, Esq., 415.630.5701, specially appearing for plaintiff Lisa Ashby.
Arameh O'Boyle, Esq., 213.426.6900, for defendant Stryker Corporation.

RULING: No argument. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT STRYKER CORPORATION'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is granted as to the allegations of a design defect in the first cause of action pursuant to Plaintiff's non-opposition and is moot as to punitive damages in light of the Court's ruling on Defendant's Demurrer. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.
